patch.value:
# Story — Beyond-PM domains on pm SDK primitives

As a **platform developer**, I can model a **non-PM domain system** — for example a git-like version-control workflow (changesets, refs, merges) or a company's business processes (approvals, invoicing, HR flows) — using **only public pm SDK primitives**, so the SDK proves itself as a universal, event-sourced application platform rather than a PM-only toolkit.

## Why
The product vision (ADR pm-oxq5) states the SDK should be extensible enough to build "an entire SCV like Git or entire companies and their business logic". Today every SDK story/exemplar (pm-w89s, pm-cbwg) stays inside the PM domain. Nothing exercises — or even inventories — the primitive set a foreign domain needs: fully custom item types and status graphs, domain verbs via extension commands, transactional multi-item mutations, event-sourced history replay as domain state, and hooks as business rules.

## Scope (exploration story, horizon 5)
- Inventory the primitive gaps: which SDK capabilities does a non-PM domain need that the promoted primitive set (pm-rjqr/pm-98cz/pm-3mna/pm-4a7m et al.) does not yet expose?
- Feed each gap back as a concrete SDK item under pm-usfg (create-only, dedupe-checked).
- Acceptance is demonstrated by the exemplar spike child, not by shipping a production VCS.

## Out of scope
Building a real VCS or ERP; performance parity with git; any core storage change (scale work belongs to pm-9rxu).

## Sequencing
After the PM-domain exemplar pm-cbwg proves the custom-PM-tool story, and after the schema/config SDK promotion pm-3mna. Parked at P4 with pm-is1a (federation) in horizon 5.
As a platform developer, I can build a non-PM domain system (e.g. a git-like VCS or a company's business workflows) using only pm SDK primitives

Horizon-5 exploration story: prove the pm SDK is a universal event-sourced application platform, not a PM-only toolkit. Inventories the primitive gaps a foreign domain exposes and feeds them back as concrete SDK items. Dedupe-checked 2026-07-06: pm search for VCS/business-logic/beyond-PM coverage found only the vision ADR pm-oxq5 and PM-domain stories pm-w89s/pm-cbwg; no item tracks the beyond-PM ambition.

Primitive-gap inventory exists for at least one non-PM domain; every gap is filed as an individual dedupe-checked SDK item under pm-usfg or explicitly waived; exemplar spike child demonstrates the story end-to-end

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Maintainer re-affirmed 2026-07-11: this story is an explicit product goal (build a git-like VCS or an entire company's business logic on pm SDK primitives alone). Plan of record lives in the SDK-DX map note on epic pm-usfg (2026-07-11) — primitive checklist with owners, and the sequencing rule: the pm-xtrd changeset-exemplar spike runs AFTER the six promotion slices land and is the honest detector of missing primitives; no speculative SDK features ahead of it. Acceptance sketch for this story: the spike's package imports only public SDK entry points (boundary gate pm-8778 proves it), defines its own item types/statuses via the schema primitives, and replays its full event history through the immutable history stream.

author: codex-root
message: Relationship audit 2026-07-13: the VCS child is the story acceptance vehicle; link the existing company-policy exploration as adjacent business-domain proof

author: codex-sdk-vcs-20260718
message: Broad SDK/VCS lane: prove the universal non-PM SDK story through the VCS exemplar

message: Start universal SDK acceptance proof and primitive gap classification

value.author: codex-sdk-vcs-20260718
value.text: Implementation start 2026-07-18: canonical child pm-xtrd is now active as the acceptance vehicle; pm-ju83 is active only for the durable graph/event primitives the exemplar concretely exercises. No speculative or duplicate SDK item was created.

value.note: Non-PM domain primitive inventory and explicit waiver record

value.author: codex-sdk-vcs-20260718
value.text: Story tranche evidence 2026-07-18: the VCS acceptance vehicle proves a non-PM domain using only public SDK primitives and includes an itemized GAP_REPORT.md. Every observed primitive need is either complete, explicitly waived as product-specific/bounded, or retained on canonical existing graph/scale lineage; no speculative duplicate SDK items were created. Linked acceptance test passes. The separate temporal digital-twin child pm-kr3t remains open, so this broader story returns to open rather than closing over an active future exemplar.

value.author: codex-sdk-vcs-20260718
value.text: Project management = context management generalizes cleanly: custom entities, lifecycle, business rules, point-in-time state, graph semantics, and derived event projections can be composed as package-owned domain context while core supplies governed storage and execution primitives.

author: codex-sdk-vcs-20260718
message: VCS acceptance tranche complete; retain story for open temporal digital-twin child

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Story acceptance evidence 2026-07-18 (PR#587): exemplar child pm-xtrd CLOSED — @unbrained/pm-vcs models the full changeset/ref domain on public SDK primitives only (custom types+fields, schema-owned lifecycle, seven domain verbs, reviewed-merge hook, event-sourced history with time travel, durable commits_to stream + projections, crash-consistent merge reconciliation). Primitive-gap inventory = packages/pm-vcs/GAP_REPORT.md; every row Complete, explicitly waived (atomic multi-item+relationship transaction boundary — tracked separately under pm-usfg), or out-of-scope (content-addressed storage/tree diff/transport = VCS-product concerns). Gaps surfaced during the lane filed as pm-v1yo (alias collision diagnostics) + pm-um4g (audit unit consistency). All three AC clauses met.

author: maintainer-agent

patch.value: Story accepted via the pm-xtrd exemplar (PR#587): @unbrained/pm-vcs proves a non-PM domain runs on public SDK primitives alone; primitive-gap inventory complete with every gap shipped, filed (pm-v1yo, pm-um4g, pm-4e12 transaction boundary), or out-of-scope.
A platform developer can model a foreign domain (VCS changesets/refs) using only public pm SDK primitives, with a gap inventory feeding filed or waived SDK items.

packages/pm-vcs ships custom types/fields, schema-owned lifecycle, seven domain commands, reviewed-merge hook, event-sourced history with time travel, durable relationship-event projections, and crash-consistent merge reconciliation — zero private core imports, packed-artifact acceptance at 100% coverage; GAP_REPORT.md dispositions all twelve domain needs.

message: Record acceptance triple ahead of close

author: maintainer-agent

message: Accepted 2026-07-18: exemplar child pm-xtrd shipped via PR#587 (@unbrained/pm-vcs on public SDK only), GAP_REPORT.md inventory complete, residual gaps tracked as pm-v1yo/pm-um4g/pm-4e12.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Active acceptance tranche evidence: the pm-vcs exemplar no longer carries its transaction waiver. A package using only public @unbrained/pm-cli/sdk and extension-host services atomically coordinates a Changeset lifecycle transition with an append-only commits_to relationship, including compensation and crash replay. Packed temp install and full VCS flow passed. The separate temporal/business-workflow child remains outside this PR, so this broad story is not being closed by this tranche.

Compensation restores original refs and preserves concurrent newer state. The temporal digital-twin child remains outside this tranche, so this story returns to open.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Phase-two acceptance evidence: @unbrained/pm-digital-twin is a non-project-management facility domain implemented through public SDK contracts, with custom item types/fields, typed relationship ontology, immutable observations, event-time replay, graph invariants, impact paths, offline merge, checkpointed export/import, and a complete clean installed-CLI workflow.

patch.value: Accepted the universal non-PM domain story with both VCS and temporal digital-twin exemplars using public SDK contracts only; gap reports, clean installed workflows, strict gates, and complete coverage provide end-to-end evidence.
